title: Marrow Cell Type Annotation
summary: Annotate every cell in a staged Smart-seq2 mouse bone marrow AnnData object using a fixed 21-label ontology and return one predicted label per cell.
taskPrompt:
You are given an unlabeled Smart-seq2 mouse bone marrow single-cell dataset and must annotate every cell with one label from a fixed 21-class ontology.

Task directory:
- `/media/user/data/agenthle/life_sciences/tms_marrow_cell_type_annotation_instance_1/base`

Visible inputs:
- AnnData matrix: `/media/user/data/agenthle/life_sciences/tms_marrow_cell_type_annotation_instance_1/base/input/tms_marrow_unlabeled.h5ad`
- Allowed labels: `/media/user/data/agenthle/life_sciences/tms_marrow_cell_type_annotation_instance_1/base/input/allowed_labels.txt`
- Agent-facing runtime manifest: `/media/user/data/agenthle/life_sciences/tms_marrow_cell_type_annotation_instance_1/base/input/runtime_env/pyproject.toml`
- Agent-facing runtime lockfile: `/media/user/data/agenthle/life_sciences/tms_marrow_cell_type_annotation_instance_1/base/input/runtime_env/uv.lock`
- Task note: `/media/user/data/agenthle/life_sciences/tms_marrow_cell_type_annotation_instance_1/base/input/input.txt`

Recommended setup:
- Work on Linux in `/media/user/data/agenthle/life_sciences/tms_marrow_cell_type_annotation_instance_1/base`
- If you need Scanpy / AnnData tooling, install from the staged runtime with:
  `uv sync --frozen --project "/media/user/data/agenthle/life_sciences/tms_marrow_cell_type_annotation_instance_1/base/input/runtime_env"`

Required output:
- Write exactly one UTF-8 CSV to `/media/user/data/agenthle/life_sciences/tms_marrow_cell_type_annotation_instance_1/base/output/predictions.csv`
- Keep the header exactly `cell_id,predicted_cell_type`
- Emit exactly one row for every input cell
- Use only labels from `/media/user/data/agenthle/life_sciences/tms_marrow_cell_type_annotation_instance_1/base/input/allowed_labels.txt`

Relevant metadata available in the AnnData object includes:
- `age`
- `mouse.id`
- `sex`
- `n_genes`
- `n_counts`

This task is graded against hidden reference labels. A submission counts as successful only if it is format-valid and achieves both:
- macro F1 >= 0.75
- overall accuracy >= 0.85

Do not ask for confirmation. Execute directly.
agentMustDo: Load the staged Smart-seq2 marrow AnnData object on Linux and, if needed, install the staged Python runtime from `input/runtime_env/` with `uv`.
Run a biologically reasonable batch-aware single-cell annotation workflow across the fixed 21-label ontology.
Write exactly one UTF-8 CSV to `output/predictions.csv` with header `cell_id,predicted_cell_type` and one row per input cell.

inputFiles.name: tms_marrow_unlabeled.h5ad
inputFiles.description: Unlabeled Smart-seq2 mouse bone marrow AnnData object with 14,517 cells.

inputFiles.name: input.txt
inputFiles.description: Short task note summarizing the runtime contract.

inputFiles.name: pyproject.toml
inputFiles.description: Agent-facing Python runtime manifest for Scanpy / AnnData tooling.

inputFiles.name: uv.lock
inputFiles.description: Locked dependency resolution for the staged runtime.

evaluation:
- Hard-fail conditions: missing CSV, wrong header, wrong row count, duplicate or missing `cell_id`, extra `cell_id`, or unsupported labels.
- Valid submissions are matched to hidden ground truth by exact `cell_id`.
- The evaluator computes macro F1 across all 21 labels and overall accuracy across all cells.
- `score = 1.0` only if `macro F1 >= 0.75` and `overall accuracy >= 0.85`; otherwise `score = 0.0`.
